name: aoc-helper

metadata.name: aoc-helper
metadata.summary: A helper package for Advent of Code

documentation:
# `aoc_helper`

[![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black)

@salt-die's aoc_helper package, rewritten from the ground up

## Automation

This project aims to be compliant with the [Advent of Code Automation Guidelines](https://www.reddit.com/r/adventofcode/wiki/faqs/automation). Here are the strategies it uses:

- Once inputs are downloaded, they are cached in `~/.config/aoc_helper/YEAR/DAY.in` (or a similar path for Windows users) - [`interface.fetch`](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L97-L155) (lines [107-108](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L107-L108), [152](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L152))
- The `User-Agent` header declares the package name, version, and my contact info - [`data.HEADERS`](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/data.py#L20-L25), [used](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L118) [in](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L139) [every](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L210) [outbound](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L266) [request](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L341)
- If requesting input before the puzzle unlocks, [the library will wait for unlock before sending any requests](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L130-L133) (except on day 1, where it will send a request to validate the session token)
- If sending an answer too soon after an incorrect one, [the library will wait the cooldown specified in the response](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L231-L234) (sending only one extra request; it *is* however possible for a user to send multiple requests in quick succession, by repeatedly calling `submit` before the cooldown is over)
- Advent of Code will not be queried at all [if the puzzle has already been solved](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L182-L190) or [if an answer has already been submitted](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/interface.py#L193-L199)
- If, for some reason, the user decides they wish to clear their cache (for example, if they believe their input to be corrupted) they can do so by using the [`aoc clean`](https://github.com/Starwort/aoc_helper/blob/master/aoc_helper/main.py#L91-L121) command.

## Installation

Install `aoc_helper` with `pip`!

```bash
pip install aoc_helper
# install the dependencies required for the Command Line Interface
pip install aoc_helper[cli]
# install the dependencies required for colour
pip install aoc_helper[fancy]
# install all additional dependencies
pip install aoc_helper[cli,fancy]
# or
pip install aoc_helper[full]
```

## Configuration

When you first use any function that interfaces with Advent of Code, you will be prompted to enter your session token.

Your session token is stored as a *HTTPOnly cookie*. This means there is no way of extracting it with JavaScript, you either must
use a browser extension such as [EditThisCookie](http://www.editthiscookie.com/), or follow [this guide](https://github.com/wimglenn/advent-of-code-wim/issues/1)

This token is stored in `~/.config/aoc_helper/token.txt` (`C:\Users\YOUR_USERNAME\.config\aoc_helper\token.txt` on Windows,
probably), and other `aoc_helper` data is stored in this folder (such as your input and submission caches).

If, for whatever reason, you feel the need to clear your caches, you can do so by deleting the relevant folders in `aoc_helper`'s
configuration folder.

## Command Line Interface

`aoc_helper` has a command line interface, accessed by running `python -m aoc_helper` or `aoc` followed by the command line arguments. Its commands are detailed below:

### `fetch`

`aoc fetch <DAY> [--year <YEAR>]`

Fetch your input for a given day.  
YEAR is the current year by default.

Examples (written during 2020):

```bash
aoc fetch 2 # fetches input for 2020 day 2
aoc fetch 24 --year 2019 # fetches input for 2019 day 24
```

### `submit`

`aoc submit <DAY> <PART> <ANSWER> [--year <YEAR>]`

Submits your answer for a given day and part.  
YEAR is the current year by default.

Examples (written during 2020):

```bash
aoc submit 2 1 643 # submits 643 as the answer for 2020 day 2 part 1
aoc submit 24 1 12531574 --year 2019 # submits 12531574 as the answer for 2019 day 2 part 1
```

### `template`

`aoc template <DAYS> [--year YEAR]`

Generates templates for your advent of code folder.  
YEAR is the current year by default.

`DAYS` must be a comma-separated list of date ranges, which may be one of:

- `all`, to generate a template for every day in the year
- A single integer in the range \[1, 25] to generate a single template
- A pair of integers in the range \[1, 25], separated with a hyphen, to generate template for each day in the range

Note that even if a day is included in the list of days more than once (including implicitly, within ranges or `all`), it will only be generated once.

Filenames are formatted as `day_NUMBER.py` where `NUMBER` is the 2-digit day number.

Examples (written during 2020):

```bash
aoc template all # generates day_01.py to day_25.py, with aoc_helper methods referencing 2020, in the current folder
aoc template 3 --year 2019 # generates day_03.py, with aoc_helper methods referencing 2019, in the current folder
aoc template 3-5 --year 2017 # generates day_03.py to day_05.py, with aoc_helper methods referencing 2017, in the current folder
aoc template 3-5,7,9,9-10 # generates files for days 3, 4, 5, 7, 9, and 10
```

functions.name: aoc_helper.day_template.part_one
documentation: providing this default is somewhat of a hack - there isn't any other way to
force type inference to happen, AFAIK - but this won't work with standard
collections (list, set, dict, tuple)

functions.name: aoc_helper.day_template.part_two
documentation: providing this default is somewhat of a hack - there isn't any other way to
force type inference to happen, AFAIK - but this won't work with standard
collections (list, set, dict, tuple)

variables.name: aoc_helper.types.SupportsRichComparisonT
documentation: noqa: Y001

functions.name: aoc_helper.main.fetch
documentation: Fetch and print the input for day DAY of --year

functions.name: aoc_helper.main.read
documentation: Read the puzzle for day DAY or --year in your terminal

functions.name: aoc_helper.main.submit
documentation: Submit the answer for day DAY part PART of --year

functions.name: aoc_helper.main.template
documentation: Generate an answer stub for every day of DAYS in --year

functions.name: aoc_helper.main.browser
documentation: Enable, disable, or check browser automation

functions.name: aoc_helper.main.clean
documentation: Clean the cached --type data for DAYS of YEAR

functions.name: aoc_helper.main.visualise_cache
documentation: Get a visual overview of the aoc_helper cache

functions.name: aoc_helper.main.validate_token
documentation:
Validate the stored session token. Will prompt for a new token if the
current one is invalid.

functions.name: aoc_helper.main.practice_results
documentation: Show all practice results for day DAY of --year

functions.name: aoc_helper.interface._open_page
documentation: Open the page if the user hasn't opted out

functions.name: aoc_helper.interface._make
documentation: Create folder if it doesn't exist.

functions.name: aoc_helper.interface._pretty_print
documentation: Analyse and print message

functions.name: aoc_helper.interface.fetch
documentation:
Fetch and return the input for `day` of `year`.

If `--practice` is provided on the command line, pretend that today is the
day of the puzzle and wait for puzzle unlock accordingly. 'today' is
determined by UTC; from 0:00 to 5:00 UTC, this puzzle will block until 5:00
UTC - after that, until 0:00 UTC the next day, input fetching will be
instant.

All inputs are cached in `aoc_helper.DATA_DIR`.

functions.name: aoc_helper.interface.submit
documentation:
Submit a solution.

Submissions are cached; submitting an already submitted solution will return the
previous response.

functions.name: aoc_helper.interface.lazy_submit
documentation:
Run the function only if we haven't seen a solution.

Will also run the solution if `--force-run` or `--practice` is passed on the
command line.

solution is expected to be named 'part_one' or 'part_two'

functions.name: aoc_helper.interface.get_sample_input
documentation: Retrieves the example input and answer for the corresponding AOC challenge.

functions.name: aoc_helper.interface.lazy_test
documentation:
Test the function with AOC's example data only if we haven't tested it already.

Solution is expected to be named 'part_one' or 'part_two'

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.extract_ints
documentation:
Utility function to extract all integers from some string.

Some inputs can be directly parsed with this function.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.extract_uints
documentation:
Utility function to extract all integers from some string.

Minus signs will be *ignored*; the output integers will all be positive.

Some inputs can be directly parsed with this function.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.extract_ranges
documentation:
Utility function to extract all ranges from some string.

Ranges are interpreted as `start-stop` and are not inclusive.

Some inputs can be directly parsed with this function.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.extract_iranges
documentation:
Utility function to extract all ranges from some string.

Ranges are interpreted as `start-stop` and are inclusive.

Some inputs can be directly parsed with this function.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.chunk
documentation:
Utility function to chunk an iterable into chunks of a given size.

If there are not enough elements in the iterable to fill the last chunk,
the last chunk will be dropped.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.chunk_default
documentation:
Utility function to chunk an iterable into chunks of a given size.

If there are not enough elements in the iterable to fill the last chunk,
the missing elements will be replaced with the default value.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.irange
documentation:
Inclusive range. Returns an iterator that
yields values from start to stop, including both
endpoints, stepping by one. Works even when
stop < start (the iterator will step backwards).

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.frange
documentation:
Float range. Returns an iterator that yields values
from start (inclusive) to stop (exclusive), changing by step.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.tail_call
documentation:
Add tail recursion optimisation to func.

Useful for avoiding RecursionErrors.

This is done by throwing an exception
if the wrapper is its own grandparent (i.e. the wrapped
function would be its own parent), and catching such
exceptions to fake the tail call optimisation.

func will behave strangely if the decorated
function recurses in a non-tail context.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.encode_letter
documentation:
Encode a matrix of dots to an integer for efficient
storage and lookup. Not expected to be used outside of
this module and contributions to the lookup table.

The matrix of dots should be 6 tall and 5 wide.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.decode_letter
documentation:
Decode a matrix of dots to a single letter.

The matrix of dots should be 6 tall and 5 wide.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.decode_text
documentation:
Decode a matrix of dots to text.

The matrix of dots should be 6 tall and 5n - 1 wide.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.points_between
documentation:
Return an iterator of points between start and end, inclusive.
Start to end must be horizontal, vertical, or a perfect diagonal.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.expect
documentation: Expect that a value is not None.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.narrow_list
documentation:
Narrow the type of list based on the passed type.

Assumes that list is homogenous.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.pathfind
documentation:
Use the A* algorithm to find the best path from start to end, and
return the total cost.

start defaults to the top left, and end defaults to the bottom right.

grid is assumed to be a rectangular 2D array of integers, *not* a ragged
array. Bad things will happen if you pass a ragged array.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.search
documentation:
Perform A* (or Dijkstra if heuristic is not provided) search on a state
space, returning the number of steps and all states in the chosen path
(including both start and end point).

Will optimise to avoid revisiting seen states if the state type is hashable,
or if the freeze function is provided. The freeze function will take
priority over the default hashing behaviour for the state type, if present.

It is probably a good idea to either make your state type hashable, or
provide a freeze function.

functions.name: aoc_helper.utils.chinese_remainder_theorem
documentation:
Given the numbers N % modulus_i = residue_i, return N % prod(modulus_i).

Moduli must be pairwise coprime (i.e. no pair of moduli may share a factor
other than 1) - violating this constraint will produce an undefined result.

classes.name: aoc_helper.utils.list

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.iter
documentation: Return an iterator over the list.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.mapped
documentation:
Return a list containing the result of calling func on each
element in the list. The function is called on each element immediately.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.starmapped
documentation:
Return a list containing the result of calling func on each
element in the list. The function is called on each element immediately.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.mapped_each
documentation:
Return a list containing the results of mapping each element of self
with func. The function is called on each element immediately.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.starmapped_each
documentation:
Return a list containing the results of mapping each element of self
with func. The function is called on each element immediately.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.filtered
documentation:
Return a list containing only the elements for which pred
returns True.

If pred is None, return a list containing only elements that are
truthy.

If pred is a T (and T is not a callable or None), return a list
containing only elements that compare equal to pred.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.find
documentation:
Return the first element of self for which pred returns True.

If pred is None, return the first element which is truthy.

If pred is a T (and T is not a callable or None), return the first element
that compares equal to pred.

If no such element exists, return None.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.any
documentation:
Consume this iterator and return True if any element satisfies the
given predicate. The default predicate is bool; therefore by default this
method returns True if any element is truthy.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.all
documentation:
Consume this iterator and return True if all elements satisfy the
given predicate. The default predicate is bool; therefore by default this
method returns True if all elements are truthy.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.none
documentation:
Consume this iterator and return True if no element satisfies the
given predicate. The default predicate is bool; therefore by default this
method returns True if no element is truthy.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.windowed
documentation:
Return an list containing the elements of this list in
a sliding window of size window_size. If there are not enough elements
to create a full window, the list will be empty.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.shifted_zip
documentation:
Return an iterator containing pairs of elements separated by shift.

If there are fewer than shift elements, the iterator will be empty.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.reduce
documentation:
Reduce the list to a single value, using the reduction
function provided.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.accumulated
documentation:
Return the accumulated results of calling func on the elements in
this list.

initial is only usable on versions of Python equal to or greater than 3.8.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.chunked
documentation:
Return a list containing the elements of this list in chunks
of size n. If there are not enough elements to fill the last chunk, it
will be dropped.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.chunked_default
documentation:
Return a list containing the elements of this list in chunks
of size n. If there are not enough elements to fill the last chunk, the
missing elements will be replaced with the default value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.sum
documentation:
Return the sum of all elements in this list.

If initial is provided, it is used as the initial value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.prod
documentation:
Return the product of all elements in this list.

If initial is provided, it is used as the initial value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.sorted
documentation:
Return a list containing the elements of this list sorted
according to the given key and reverse parameters.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.reversed
documentation:
Return a list containing the elements of this list in
reverse order.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.min
documentation:
Return the minimum element of this list, according to the given
key.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.max
documentation:
Return the maximum element of this list, according to the given
key.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.len
documentation: Return the length of this list.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.mean
documentation:
Statistical mean of this list.

T must be summable and divisible by an integer,
and there must be at least one element in this list.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.median
documentation:
Statistical median of this list.

T must be orderable and there must be at least one
element in this list.
Further more, if this list contains an odd number
of elements, T must also be summable and divisible
by an integer.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.mode
documentation:
Statistical mode of this list.

T must be hashable and there must be at least one
element in this list.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.flat
documentation:
Flattened version of this list.

If recursive is specified, flattens recursively instead
of by one layer.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.nlargest
documentation: Return the n largest elements of self.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.nsmallest
documentation: Return the n smallest elements of self.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.transposition
documentation:
Return the transposition of this list, which is assumed to be
rectangular, not ragged. If this list was ragged, then it will be
cropped to the largest rectangle that is fully populated.

This operation looks similar to a 90° rotation followed by a reflection:

ABC
DEF
HIJ
KLM

transposes to:

ADHK
BEIL
CFJM

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.into_grid
documentation:
Convert this list, which is assumed to be rectangular, not ragged,
into a Grid.

This function converts directly; it doesn't copy - expect strange
behaviour if you continue using self.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.into_queue
documentation:
Convert this list into a PrioQueue.

This function converts directly; it doesn't copy - expect strange
behaviour if you continue using self.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.into_builtin
documentation:
Unwrap this list into a builtins.list.

This function converts directly; it doesn't copy - expect strange
behaviour if you continue using self.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.combinations
documentation:
Return a list over the combinations, without replacement, of
length r of the elements of this list.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.combinations_with_replacement
documentation:
Return a list over the combinations, with replacement, of
length r of the elements of this list.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.permutations
documentation:
Return a list over the permutations of the elements of this
list.

If r is provided, the returned list will only contain permutations
of size r.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.list.divide
documentation: Divide this list into n equal-sized chunks.
Smart/fluent list class

classes.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.map
documentation:
Return an iterator containing the result of calling func on each
element in this iterator.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.starmap
documentation:
Return an iterator containing the result of calling func on each
element in this iterator.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.map_each
documentation:
Return an iterator containing the result of calling func on each
element in each element in this iterator.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.starmap_each
documentation:
Return an iterator containing the result of calling func on each
element in each element in this iterator.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.filter
documentation:
Return an iterator containing only the elements for which pred
returns True.

If pred is a T (and T is not callable), return an iterator
containing only elements that compare equal to pred.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.find
documentation:
Return the first element of self for which pred returns True.

If pred is None, return the first element which is truthy.

If pred is a T (and T is not a callable or None), return the first element
that compares equal to pred.

If no such element exists, return None.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.any
documentation:
Consume this iterator and return True if any element satisfies the
given predicate. The default predicate is bool; therefore by default this
method returns True if any element is truthy.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.all
documentation:
Consume this iterator and return True if all elements satisfy the
given predicate. The default predicate is bool; therefore by default this
method returns True if all elements are truthy.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.none
documentation:
Consume this iterator and return True if no element satisfies the
given predicate. The default predicate is bool; therefore by default this
method returns True if no element is truthy.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.reduce
documentation:
Reduce the iterator to a single value, using the reduction
function provided.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.accumulate
documentation:
Return the accumulated results of calling func on the elements in
this iterator.

initial is only usable on versions of Python equal to or greater than 3.8.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.foreach
documentation: Run func on every value in this iterator, immediately.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.chunk
documentation:
Return an iterator containing the elements of this iterator in chunks
of size n. If there are not enough elements to fill the last chunk, it
will be dropped.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.chunk_default
documentation:
Return an iterator containing the elements of this iterator in chunks
of size n. If there are not enough elements to fill the last chunk, the
missing elements will be replaced with the default value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.window
documentation:
Return an iterator containing the elements of this iterator in
a sliding window of size window_size. If there are not enough elements
to create a full window, the iterator will be empty.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.shifted_zip
documentation:
Return an iterator containing pairs of elements separated by shift.

If there are fewer than shift elements, the iterator will be empty.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.next
documentation: Return the next element in the iterator, or raise StopIteration.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.next_or
documentation: Return the next element in the iterator, or default.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.skip
documentation:
Skip and discard n elements from this iterator.

Raises StopIteration if there are not enough elements.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.nth
documentation:
Return the nth element of this iterator.

Discards all elements up to the nth element, and raises StopIteration
if there are not enough elements.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.take
documentation:
Return the next n elements of this iterator.

Raises StopIteration if there are not enough elements.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.collect
documentation: Return a list containing all remaining elements of this iterator.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.chain
documentation:
Return an iterator containing the elements of this iterator followed
by the elements of other.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.sum
documentation:
Return the sum of all elements in this iterator.

If initial is provided, it is used as the initial value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.prod
documentation:
Return the product of all elements in this iterator.

If initial is provided, it is used as the initial value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.sorted
documentation:
Return a list containing the elements of this iterator sorted
according to the given key and reverse parameters.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.reversed
documentation:
Return an iterator containing the elements of this iterator in
reverse order.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.min
documentation:
Return the minimum element of this iterator, according to the given
key.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.max
documentation:
Return the maximum element of this iterator, according to the given
key.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.tee
documentation:
Return a tuple of n iterators containing the elements of this
iterator.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.permutations
documentation:
Return an iterator over the permutations of the elements of this
iterator.

If r is provided, the returned iterator will only contain permutations
of size r.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.combinations
documentation:
Return an iterator over the combinations, without replacement, of
length r of the elements of this iterator.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.combinations_with_replacement
documentation:
Return an iterator over the combinations, with replacement, of
length r of the elements of this iterator.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.flatten
documentation:
Flatten this iterator.

If recursive is specified, flattens recursively instead
of by one layer.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.enumerate
documentation:
Return an iterator over the elements of this iterator, paired with
their index, starting at start.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.count
documentation: Consume this iterator and return the number of elements it contained.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.nlargest
documentation: Consume this iterator and return the n largest elements.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.iter.nsmallest
documentation: Consume this iterator and return the n smallest elements.
Smart/fluent iterator class

classes.name: aoc_helper.utils.multirange
documentation:
Multirange class. Represents many disjoint ranges of integers. Step sizes
must always be 1.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.from_string
documentation:
Create a grid from a string (e.g. a puzzle input).

Can take a classifier to use a custom classification. The default will
map numbers from 0 to 9 to themselves, and . and # to 0 and 1 respectively.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.vertical_chunks
documentation:
Create a list of grids formed by splitting this grid every n rows.

Any extra rows that cannot form a group of n will be lost (see
vertical_chunks_default)

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.vertical_chunks_default
documentation:
Create a list of grids formed by splitting this grid every n rows.

Grids will be padded out to have n rows, where every cell in the padded
rows is fill_value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.horizontal_chunks
documentation:
Create a list of grids formed by splitting this grid every n columns.

Any extra columns that cannot form a group of n will be lost (see
horizontal_chunks_default)

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.horizontal_chunks_default
documentation:
Create a list of grids formed by splitting this grid every n columns.

Rows will be padded out to have n values, where every cell in the padded
columns is fill_value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.transpose
documentation:
Create a grid that is the transposition of this grid.

This operation looks similar to a 90° rotation followed by a reflection:

ABC
DEF
HIJ
KLM

transposes to:

ADHK
BEIL
CFJM

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.rotate_clockwise
documentation:
Create a new grid that is the clockwise rotation of this grid.

self[0][0] is considered to be the top-left corner.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.rotate_anticlockwise
documentation:
Create a new grid that is the anti-clockwise rotation of this grid.

self[0][0] is considered to be the top-left corner.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.to_bool_grid
documentation:
Create a new grid of booleans by using the given conversion function
on self. The default conversion function is bool, converting via
truthiness value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.decode_as_text
documentation:
Decode self as a grid of letters using decode_text.

This method will check that self is the correct dimensions and raise an
AssertionError if not.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.trim_to_content
documentation:
Create a new grid of booleans by using the given conversion function
on self. The default conversion function is bool, converting via
truthiness value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.neighbours
documentation:
Return the neighbours of a point in the grid (but not the point itself).

Examples below:
- A is the point (x, y)
- * are points returned
- . are other points in the grid

...........
..***......
..*A*......
..***......

A*.........
**.........
...........
...........

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.orthogonal_neighbours
documentation:
Return the orthogonal neighbours of a point in the grid (but not the
point itself).

Examples below:
- A is the point (x, y)
- * are points returned
- . are other points in the grid

...........
...*.......
..*A*......
...*.......

A*.........
*..........
...........
...........

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.Grid.pathfind
documentation:
Use the A* algorithm to find the best path from start to end, and
return the total cost.

start defaults to the top left, and end defaults to the bottom right.

initial_state is for custom state for the pathfinding algorithm (e.g.
extra restrictions on the path). State must be a hashable type

is_valid_end is a function that takes in a state and returns whether the
search can end with that state. It will only be called if the target
position has been found.

next_state is a function that takes the current state, the change in the
x and y coordinates, the previous cell value, and the current cell
value, and returns either the next state or None if the traversal cannot
be performed.

cost_function is a function that takes the start value and the end value
of a traversal, and returns the cost of that traversal. The default is
that the cost is the difference between the two values.

neighbour_type is either "ortho" or "full", and determines whether
diagonal traversals are considered. The default is "ortho", meaning no
diagonal traversals will be considered in the solution.

initial_cost is the zero-value of the cell type. You should only need to
modify this if you're using a non-numeric type.

heuristic_multiplier is a multiplier applied to the heuristic function.
The heuristic function will be either Manhattan distance from the
current state to the goal (in "ortho" mode) or Euclidean distance from
the current state to the goal (in "full" mode). The default is 1, which
means that the heuristic function will be used as-is. A value of 0 will
devolve the search to Dijkstra's algorithm, and a value higher than 1
may improve search time, potentially at the cost of accuracy.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.SparseGrid.draw_line
documentation:
Draw a line on a sparse grid, setting all points between start and end
to value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.SparseGrid.draw_lines
documentation:
Draw a series of lines on a sparse grid, setting all points between
each pair of points to value.

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.SparseGrid.bounds
documentation: Return the bounds of a sparse grid, as a tuple of (min_x, min_y, max_x, max_y).

methods.name: aoc_helper.utils.SparseGrid.pretty_print
documentation: Print a sparse grid to the console.
